vault.errTimeoutAction: Try again — if it keeps timing out, test one provider at a time via the CLI.
vault.errServerUnavailableDetail: The aikey-local-server returned an internal error. The auto-retry already tried once. Usually a quick restart fixes it.

trustCheck.title: Trust Check

on.label: OBSERVER ON
on.title: Watching user_chat through the proxy — D-rules (D4/D5/D6) score every chunk in real time.

standby.label: OBSERVER STANDBY
standby.title: trust-local is up but real-time detection is OFF — only manual Check runs D-rules. Flip the Real-time toggle on the right to watch user_chat.

offline.label: OBSERVER OFFLINE
offline.title: trust-local is down — neither manual Check nor real-time will work. Click Start service below or run aikey service start trust-local in a terminal.

trustCheck.subtitle: Trust signals across your provider sources · powered by trust-local
trustCheck.runChecksTitle: Trigger a Check run for every row in the table
trustCheck.notInstalledDetail: in a terminal to install the trust-local service (~23MB binary, runs on 127.0.0.1:8801).
trustCheck.offlineRelaunchTail: in a terminal. The page auto-recovers on next 30s tick.
trustCheck.bandSortHint: BAND is sorted by latest detection time, not by rank.
trustCheck.disclaimerReplicate: Replicate ≥ 3 times and look for a consistently low layer before treating an anomaly as evidence.
trustCheck.disclaimerDataLocalTail: Degrade detection does not upload your conversations, KEYs, or Check results.
trustCheck.disclaimerNoCertBold: not certify or de-certify any provider
trustCheck.disclaimerNoCertTail: Scores reflect this run's measurement against a healthy baseline; they are informational, not endorsements or accusations.
trustCheck.disclaimerHeuristicTail: Verify independently before taking action (refund, switching providers, public posts).
trustCheck.disclaimerAsIs: Tool provided as-is. AiKey is not liable for decisions made on the basis of these results.
trustCheck.offlineNoDataTitle: No data while trust-local is offline.
trustCheck.offlineNoDataNote: Rows refresh automatically once the service is back up.
trustCheck.emptyNote: Send a request through aikey-proxy — once trust-local sees one observation it shows up here automatically.
trustCheck.filteredEmptyTitle: No rows match the current filter.
trustCheck.filteredEmptyNotePrefix: Try toggling a chip off, broadening the search, or
trustCheck.baseUrlEmptyNote: Once trust-local sees credentials with a base_url they appear here, deduped by gateway.
trustCheck.baseUrlByBaseUrlNote: One row per unique gateway. Aliases sharing a base URL collapse into a single row — vault is the single source of truth.
trustCheck.baseUrlRowTitle: Click to view cascade history for representative alias
trustCheck.realtimeOnTitle: Real-time detection ON — D-rules (D4/D5/D6) run on every user chat through the proxy. Click to disable. Up to 5s before the proxy picks up the change.
trustCheck.realtimeOffTitle: Real-time detection OFF (default). Click to enable D-rules (D4/D5/D6) on every user chat through the proxy. Up to 5s before the proxy picks up the change. Per-chat overhead: ~5-10ns per SSE chunk.
trustCheck.checkActionTitle: Trigger a fresh Check run (POST /v1/verify) — manual is unlimited per M6 decision 3.1
trustCheck.verifyFailFallback: Cascade verify came back as fail.
trustCheck.verifyFailedFallback: Cascade verify came back as failed.
trustCheck.verifyErrorFallback: Cascade verify hit an error before completing — see message.
trustCheck.verifyInconclusiveFallback: Cascade verify was inconclusive — retry to refine the score.
trustCheck.removeInUseHint: Currently in use — switch with `aikey use` to another alias first to remove.
trustCheck.removeNowInUseHint: Alias is now in use; switch with `aikey use` first, then retry.
trustCheck.removeArmedHint: This clears detection history for this alias. The credential in your vault is preserved.
trustCheck.removeInUseButtonTitle: Currently in use — switch via `aikey use` first
trustCheck.l1Hint: A/B/C/D5 rules on Check probe responses
trustCheck.l1Description:
How: 5 deterministic rules run against each of the 10 Check probe responses — A) domain allowlist, B) anthropic-ratelimit-* headers present, C) body shape matches Anthropic schema, D) model field consistent across SSE chunks, D5) chunk metadata complete.
Score: average pass rate across all rule × probe cells, scaled to 0–100.
Read: ≥80 healthy; 60–80 one rule degraded (often D model drift); <60 strongly suggests a proxy rewriting responses or a non-Anthropic origin.
Null until you trigger Check once.
trustCheck.l2Description:
How: blended score = 0.7 × L2-content (answer correctness) + 0.3 × L2-crowd (peer consensus). The 0.7/0.3 weighting lets a correct-but-divergent answer still score high while penalising both wrong answers and outlier responses.
Score: 0–100, both inputs on the same scale.
Read: ≥80 healthy. If low, drill into L2-content vs L2-crowd below — wrong answers (L2-content) vs divergence from healthy peers (L2-crowd) imply different root causes.
Null until first Check.
trustCheck.l2ContentDescription:
How: StructuredScorer compares each of the 10 Check probe answers against the expected canonical answer (exact + fuzzy match), produces a pass_rate.
Score: pass_rate × 100.
Read: ≥80 healthy; sustained <60 means the model is not returning correct answers to deterministic prompts — either a downgraded model or aggressive prompt rewriting.
Null until first Check runs.
trustCheck.l2CrowdDescription:
How: prefers trust-central remote quorum (aggregated recent Checks from many users on the same model). When remote is empty or unreachable, falls back to this host's own past-24h Check average. The "source" badge tells you which path served the number — `remote` / `local_24h`.
Score: 0–100, alignment with peer-group average.
Read: ≥80 = aligned with peers; <70 means your responses diverge from what other users see for the same model — a strong proxy-rewrite signal that L2-content alone cannot detect (your answers can be self-consistent yet collectively wrong).
Null when both sources are empty (e.g. first-ever Check on a brand-new model + remote stub still returning null).
trustCheck.l3Description:
How: across 10 streaming probes, collects the inter-token-time (ITT) distribution and per-probe n_chunks. Compares both against the per-model baseline (shipped with the detector or learned locally on first use).
Score: 0–100, where 100 = rhythm matches baseline exactly.
Read: ≥80 healthy; 40–80 watch (network jitter can dip a real Anthropic endpoint here); <40 strongly suggests the upstream is a re-tokenising proxy or model mirror — the timing signature of inference + streaming is very hard to forge. Requires probes to actually stream; falls back to neutral 50 if no baseline exists for the model.
trustCheck.scoreHintDefault: harmonic mean of L1 / L2 / L3 (single run; replicate for confidence)
trustCheck.scoreDescription:
How: harmonic mean of L1 / L2 / L3 (non-null layers only). Harmonic mean is biased toward the lowest layer so a single weak signal still drags the headline, but never collapses to a raw MIN — which would read as a definitive verdict that one observation does not warrant.
Score: 0–100; weakest-layer raw value (MIN) is shown in the hint for advanced users.
Read: a single low run is ONE observation, not a verdict. Replicate the Check ≥3 times and look for the SAME layer to stay low before treating an anomaly as evidence. Network jitter, transient quota issues, or one bad probe can drag a single run.
Null only when no layer has produced a score yet (first-ever Check, or all probes ineligible).
trustCheck.localHint: Check probes + observations on this host
trustCheck.localDescription:
What: every Check probe (manual ③ / auto ②) plus real-time observations (① per chat) run on this machine. Stored in trust_local.sqlite, never uploaded — your prompts, answers, and KEYs stay on disk.
Feeds: L1 (protocol rules), L2-content (answer match), L3 (rhythm fingerprint). All three layer scores above are 100% local.
Status: "active" means the proxy + trust-local are running and observations are flowing into the DB.
trustCheck.trustNetDescription:
What: aggregated Check results from many AiKey users on the same model, served by the trust-central quorum endpoint. Gives L2-crowd a peer-group baseline so we can flag responses that look self-consistent but collectively wrong (the kind of drift a single host cannot detect alone).
Status: "pending P1" — this build ships a stub that always returns null. The real crowd aggregator lands in P1.
Fallback: while remote is unavailable (now, or future outages), L2-crowd auto-uses this host's own 24h Check history average. The "source" badge on the L2-crowd card tells you which path served the number.
trustCheck.checkHistoryEmptyTail: on this row to trigger one.
trustCheck.scoringEmptyNote: No scoring detail recorded — typically the verify came from the M3 mock path and only the band-level outcome was stored.
trustCheck.recentObsEmpty: No D-rule hits on this credential. Healthy traffic produces zero observations — they appear here when the proxy spots a buffer-restream signature (D4), a model-name swap (D5), or a non-Anthropic batching rhythm (D6) on chat traffic.

trustCheckLeftover.healthNoRecentChecks: No recent checks. Run Check to see health.
trustCheckLeftover.healthStable: Sources are stable across the last 24h.
trustCheckLeftover.healthMostlyStable_one: Mostly stable; {{count}} source needs a recheck.
trustCheckLeftover.healthMostlyStable_other: Mostly stable; {{count}} sources need a recheck.
trustCheckLeftover.healthMultipleFlagged: Multiple sources flagged — recheck {{count}} before bulk use.

apps.title: Connected Apps
apps.subtitleNeverReceiveKeys: These apps can call AI providers through AiKey. They never receive your real provider keys.
apps.subtitleAuthorized: Third-party agents authorized to use your AiKey-managed keys. They never receive your real provider keys.
apps.addAppTooltip: Register a third-party app — get an env block + a one-time bearer.
apps.emptyDescPre: Apps appear here once they register with AiKey. Click
apps.emptyDescMid: above to register a third-party agent (e.g.
apps.emptyDescSuffix: ), or run the CLI command below from your terminal — vendor installers also call this.
apps.healthColTooltip: Most recent app pipeline call. ✓ OK (2xx) · ⚠ Warn (4xx) · ✗ Error (5xx or upstream error) · — never called since proxy started. Data is in-memory only; restarting aikey-proxy resets the column to '—' until traffic resumes.
apps.noAppsMatchFilters: No apps match the current filters.
apps.firstPartyBadgeTooltip: Built-in AiKey component (e.g. degrade-detector). Manageable here but tightly integrated with another panel — see related sidebar entries before changing bindings.
apps.bindingsNoneWillFail: No bindings · runtime calls will fail with BINDING_NOT_FOUND
apps.firstPartyPauseTooltip: First-party app state is self-healing and cannot be paused from the UI. Stop the plugin service itself to halt traffic.
apps.firstPartyResumeTooltip: First-party app state is self-healing and cannot be resumed from the UI (the next CLI startup auto-recovers active state).
apps.firstPartyRevokeTooltip: First-party app bearer is self-healing and cannot be revoked from the UI. Uninstall the plugin itself to remove the app.
apps.revokeConfirm:
Revoke all active keys for "{{name}}"?

The agent will immediately return 401 on its next request. The app record stays — re-register to issue a new bearer.

This cannot be undone.
apps.slugMinChars: Slug must be at least 3 characters
apps.slugMaxChars: Slug must be at most 64 characters
apps.slugShapeError: Use lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens; must start with a letter (e.g. 'claude-mem')
apps.addModalDescSuffix: ) so it can call providers through AiKey without seeing your real keys.
apps.vaultNotInitialisedSuffix: to set a master password first, then come back here.
apps.unlockToRegister: Unlock the vault to register a new app. We need the master password to issue a fresh bearer and snapshot your current
apps.unlockToRegisterSuffix: selection into the new app's bindings.
apps.slugHelp: Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. 3-64 chars. Must start with a letter.
apps.displayNameHelp: Shown in the Connected Apps list. Defaults to the slug when empty.
apps.useCurrentDefaultKey: Use my current default KEY for each picked provider (snapshotted from
apps.useCurrentDefaultKeySuffix: ). You can change bindings later from the app's detail page.
apps.firstPartySlugReservedHint: (this slug is reserved for built-in AiKey apps — pick another)
apps.switchAppSuffix: . The new binding applies to the next request the agent makes.
apps.unlockToListKeys: Unlock the vault to list your saved keys and switch the binding for
apps.oauthAccountOption: OAuth account: log in via the Vault page
apps.teamKeyOption: Team key: ask your org admin to share a virtual key for {{upstream}}
apps.switchExplainer: AiKey snapshotted your default key into a per-app binding when the app registered. Switching overrides that snapshot for this app only — other apps and the CLI continue using your global default.
apps.bearerIssuedDesc: A new bearer has been issued. Configure your agent with the env block below.
apps.bearerReusedDesc: Re-registered. The existing bearer was reused (its plaintext is shown below for your records).
apps.tokenWontShowAgain: It won't be shown again. If you lose it, use
apps.tokenWontShowAgainSuffix: from this app's detail page to issue a new one.
apps.pickBlockMatchingSdk: Pick the block matching your agent's SDK. The two URL forms differ — the SDK appends its own path suffix.
apps.missingDefaultKeyExplainerPre: selection has no key for the upstream(s) above. The agent will receive a
apps.missingDefaultKeyExplainerMid: error at request time until you pick a key. Fix it from the terminal
apps.missingDefaultKeyExplainerSuffix: or from this app's detail page
apps.rotateConfirmFull:
Rotate bearer for "{{name}}"?

The agent's existing OPENAI_API_KEY will immediately become invalid. You must copy the new bearer (shown after this completes) into the agent's env, then restart the agent.
apps.revokeConfirmDetail:
Revoke "{{name}}"?

All active keys are immediately invalidated. The agent will return 401 on its next request. This cannot be undone — re-register via CLI to issue a new bearer.
apps.mutationLockedReason: This app is wired into the AiKey internal pipeline. Revoke / rotate would break trust-local and the proxy rhythm observer. Use Pause / Resume to stop it temporarily.
apps.uninstallTooltip: Remove {{name}} from your list. Deletes the app record + key bindings; previously issued tokens are kept (revoked) for audit. Re-register the same slug to restore.
apps.uninstallConfirm:
Uninstall "{{name}}"?

This will:
  • Remove the app from your list
  • Delete the app record and its key bindings
  • Retain previously issued tokens (status: revoked) for audit history
  • Your agent's own binary is NOT touched — you manage it

The agent will return 401 on its next request. To restore, re-register the same slug — a new bearer will be issued.
apps.copyBlockRestart: Copy the block matching your agent's SDK and restart it. This is shown once.
apps.keyBindingsDesc: Which provider key the agent uses for each declared upstream.
apps.followsDefaultKey: This app follows your default key.
apps.followsDefaultKeyMid: selection at request time. To change which key it uses, run
apps.noUpstreamsDeclared: No upstreams declared at register time.
apps.dynamicallyResolved: Dynamically resolved from your default key.
apps.noBindingWillFail: No binding — runtime requests will fail with BINDING_NOT_FOUND. Bind a key to fix.
apps.switchFrozenTooltip: Binding is frozen at registration (Mode B). Switching is not supported for first-party apps.
apps.usageDesc: Daily tokens and requests expose cost drift and runaway request loops.
apps.noUsageInWindow: No usage in this window. Zero-baseline rendered honestly — the bound proxy will populate this once the agent issues a request.
apps.rankedByTokens: Ranked by total tokens in this range.
apps.noModelBreakdown: No model breakdown yet — empty range.
apps.issuedBearerDesc: Active bearer tokens issued for this connected app.
apps.rotateConfirmShort:
Rotate bearer for "{{name}}"?

The agent's existing OPENAI_API_KEY will immediately become invalid.
apps.noActiveBearer: No active bearer. Re-register via CLI:
apps.olderActiveRow: Older active row (kept during rotation window). The plaintext for this key_id is no longer accessible — only the most recent active row is revealable.
apps.auditLogPlaceholder: App-scoped audit timeline will appear here once the audit endpoint is wired. The bound proxy already emits these events to the local WAL — recoverable but not surfaced here yet.

referrals.pageDescription: Share your invite link and track referrals
referrals.inviteLinkHintPrefix: Share this link with colleagues. When they sign up with
referrals.inviteLinkHintSuffix: , you'll be credited as the referrer.
referrals.emptyState: No referrals yet. Share your link to get started!

invites.pageDescription: Generate anonymous install-attribution links. Each link tracks installs back to this machine for trend analysis — internal use only.
invites.generateBody: Each click mints a fresh short URL. Anyone clicking it gets the standard install script; their first install is attributed to this machine. You must save the link yourself — we don't keep a list.
invites.saveLinkWarning: Save this link now. We will not show it again. To revoke it later, paste the invite code {{code}} below.
invites.revokeBody: Paste an invite code below to disable it. Future clicks fall back to the unattributed install path — your machine no longer gets credit.
invites.privacyNote: Each link's only identifier is an anonymous per-machine uuid stored at ~/.aikey/identity. No email or login is attached. Delete that file to reset your identity (a new identity is generated on the next install). Per spec §6.14.7: invite Top-N rankings are internal trend analysis only — not a reward leaderboard.
invites.codeNotFound: Code not found — check that you pasted it exactly
invites.codeForbidden: This code belongs to a different machine — only the creator can revoke

login.subtitle: Member Workspace Access
login.referralMessage: A team member has shared AiKey with you
login.signInPromptInvite: Run this command — your email will be pre-filled in the browser:
login.signInPromptDefault: Run this command in your terminal to authenticate:
login.consoleHintSuffix: to open the console in your browser.

sessionExpired.heading: Your session has expired
sessionExpired.instruction: Run one of the following commands in your terminal to continue:
sessionExpired.webLabel: If only this page expired (most common):
sessionExpired.loginLabel: If the above doesn't work (login token expired):
sessionExpired.webHelp: refreshes the web session token and re-opens this console.
sessionExpired.loginHelp: re-authenticates your account when the CLI login has fully expired.

cliGuide.introTitle: Finish setup and start using your AI tools.
cliGuide.introDesc: Check the local CLI, choose how AiKey should provide keys, then use Claude, Codex, Kimi, scripts, or third-party clients without exposing real provider keys.
cliGuide.setupNote: Confirm PATH, proxy, shell hook readiness, and vault state.
cliGuide.pathsNote: Pick the one that matches how you want to use AiKey today.
cliGuide.pathPersonalBlurb: Add your own provider key to the local encrypted vault.
cliGuide.pathOAuthBlurb: Use Claude, ChatGPT, or Kimi account access without an API key.
cliGuide.pathTeamBlurb: Log in to your team's control service and select an assigned key.
cliGuide.toolsNote: Install the hook once, open a new terminal, then run your normal tools.
cliGuide.cmdListDesc: View all keys and OAuth accounts.
cliGuide.cmdRouteDesc: Show base URL and API key for clients.
cliGuide.cmdTestDesc: Test every key in the vault.
cliGuide.cmdServiceDesc: Actions: start, stop, restart. Names: trust-local, web, proxy.
cliGuide.outboundNote: If GitHub or providers are unreachable without a VPN, point aikey-proxy at your local SOCKS / HTTP proxy via proxy.env.
cliGuide.outboundTwoForms: Two forms are supported. Pick whichever you find easier to remember.
cliGuide.outboundFormB: B. Paste a shell-export snippet — wrap the whole thing in single quotes:
cliGuide.outboundPitfallBody: without the surrounding <0>'...'</0> your shell (zsh/bash) reads every <1>;</1> as a command separator. Only the first <2>export</2> reaches <3>aikey</3>; the rest run inside your current shell and never touch <4>proxy.env</4>. Diagnostic: run <5>aikey env</5> afterward — if the missing keys show up under <6>"Shell env (inherited by proxy)"</6> instead of <7>"Proxy env"</7>, that's what happened.
cliGuide.outboundFootnote: <0>aikey env set</0> only writes <1>~/.aikey/proxy.env</1> — it never touches <2>active.env</2>. It merges into the existing file (no full replace) and accepts plain <3>KEY=VAL</3> pairs, optional <4>export</4> prefix, and semicolon-separated input <5>inside a quoted string</5>.
cliGuide.troubleNote: Use these when proxy, hook, vault, or sync state looks wrong.

shared.vaultNotInitialisedTitle: Vault not initialised. Set a master password first.
shared.vaultUnlockedTitle: Vault unlocked. Click Lock on /user/vault to lock manually.

notAvailable.heading: This page requires the Team Edition

inviteDialog.title: Invite Seats
inviteDialog.pendingHint: Seats will be created with pending_claim status. Share the invite link with users so they can sign in and claim their key.

wizard.title: Batch Issue Keys
wizard.seatsWithVK: {{count}} seat(s) already have an active key — selected protocol channels will be added to the existing key.
wizard.seatsWithoutVK: {{count}} seat(s) have no active key — a new Virtual Key will be issued.
wizard.selectCredsHint:
Select credentials per protocol. First selected =

Primary

, additional = Fallback.
wizard.noActiveCreds: No active credentials. Please add credentials in Provider Accounts first.

drawer.subtitle: Seat · {{status}}
drawer.noVirtualKey: No Virtual Key yet. Select this seat in bulk actions to issue one.
drawer.waitingClaim: Waiting for {{email}} to sign up and claim this seat.

virtualKeys.tokenShownOnce: This token is shown only once. Copy it now — it cannot be retrieved again.
virtualKeys.noActiveSeats: No active seats available. Invite members from the Seats tab, then ask them to run
virtualKeys.noActiveSeatsSuffix: to claim their seat before issuing a Virtual Key.
virtualKeys.channelSelectHint: Select credentials per protocol. First selected =
virtualKeys.noActiveCredentials: No active credentials. Please add credentials in Provider Accounts first.
virtualKeys.tokenPlaintextOnce: The token plaintext is shown only once at issuance or rotation.
virtualKeys.tokenGetNew: To get a new token, click Rotate below.
virtualKeys.revokeKeyDescription: The key will be immediately invalidated and cannot be undone.
virtualKeys.rotateKeyDescription: Generates a new credential mapping. The proxy will use the new credential on the next request.

bindings.noActiveCredentials: No active credentials. Please add one in Provider Accounts first.
bindings.protocolAutoFilledHint: Auto-filled from credential — you can change it manually
bindings.switchWarnAllVks: All Virtual Keys using this channel will be switched to the new credential.
bindings.switchWarnMatchingProtocol: Only credentials with matching protocol ({{protocol}}) are listed.
bindings.noCompatibleCredentials: No compatible credentials available. Add one with the same protocol in Provider Accounts.
bindings.batchSwitchWarn: All {{count}} selected channels will be switched to the chosen credential. The new credential must match each channel's protocol type.
bindings.searchPlaceholder: Search channel name / virtual key / credential...

providerAccounts.quickSelectPlaceholder: — Fill manually or pick a preset —
providerAccounts.createSuccessNext: Next: create a protocol channel to distribute this credential to seats.
providerAccounts.rotateBody: Enter the new API Key from your provider. All Virtual Keys using this credential will switch automatically on the next request.
providerAccounts.rotateWarning: This credential is referenced by {{count}} active channel. They will all use the new key immediately.
providerAccounts.rotateWarning_plural: This credential is referenced by {{count}} active channels. They will all use the new key immediately.
providerAccounts.rotateError: Rotation failed. Please check the new API Key.
providerAccounts.migrateBody: Migrate all channels from {{name}} to another credential with the same protocol.

teamKeys.title: Team Keys
teamKeys.useTitle: Route requests through this key (aikey use)
teamKeys.useHrefTitle: Set as active key — navigates to local Vault to confirm ({{url}})
teamKeys.useHrefAriaLabel: Navigate to local vault to set as active key
teamKeys.toastNowRoutingSub: Open terminals will pick up the change on next prompt
teamKeys.detailErrorRevoked: This key has been revoked or is no longer accessible.
teamKeys.routeTokenUnlockHint: Unlock your local vault to reveal

teamKeys.emptyHintPrefix: Keys your team or organisation grants you will show up here. Ask a

effectivePacks.unavailable: Compliance filter is not running on this machine.

createDialog.title: Create Compliance Pack
createDialog.statusHint: Status defaults to 'draft' — promote to audit_only or active afterwards.
createDialog.tenantIdHint: Built-in packs are typically owned by 'system' and shared across all tenants.

deleteDialog.title: Delete Pack
deleteDialog.body: Pack <0>{{name}}</0> ({{shortId}}) will be removed. All rules and phrases inside cascade-delete.
deleteDialog.activeWarning: Active packs cannot be deleted. Deprecate first.

page.title: Compliance Packs
page.description: Manage rule / phrase packs that ai-compliance-detector pulls down for runtime detection.

list.empty: No packs match the current filters.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure your workbench connection and security preferences.

account.title: Current account
account.description: The account this console is signed in with. Confirm it's the right one before changing anything below.
account.notLoggedIn: (not logged in — run `aikey login` in your terminal)

controlUrl.title: Control URL
controlUrl.description: The team server your CLI and Web Console talk to. Changing this is equivalent to running aikey account set-url in your terminal.
controlUrl.statusReadyToTest: Edit the URL above, then click Test connectivity to verify reachability.
controlUrl.statusUnchanged: URL unchanged. Edit above to switch to a different server.
controlUrl.statusSaved: ✓ Saved. Proxy collector URL also updated; refresh other tabs to pick up the new origin.
controlUrl.saveDisabledHint: Run Test connectivity first — Save unlocks after the probe succeeds.

masterPassword.title: Master Password
masterPassword.description: Changing the vault master password is only available via the terminal for safety and to ensure local decryption keys are rotated correctly. Copy the command below and run it in a terminal.

signOut.title: Sign out
signOut.description: Sign out and clear your session on this device. You will need your master password to re-authenticate. Active CLI shells with cached keys keep working until they themselves time out.

compliance.title: AI Compliance Detection
compliance.description: Scan prompts for sensitive content (secrets, personal information, non-compliant sales language) before they are forwarded. Detection runs locally — the original text never leaves this machine. Turning it off stops all scanning.
compliance.statusOn: On — prompts are scanned before forwarding.
compliance.statusApplied: ✓ Applied. The local proxy picks up the change within a few seconds.
compliance.statusLocked: Unlock your vault to change this setting.
compliance.notInstalledDesc: The AI compliance detector is not installed on this device. Install it from a terminal, then return here to turn it on.
compliance.unlockPrompt: Enter your master password to unlock and apply this change.
compliance.recordAllowHint: Off by default — only blocked / masked / warned events are recorded. Turn on to also log clean scans (uses more space).

compliancePackDetail.deprecatedReadOnly: This pack is deprecated. Metadata is read-only.
compliancePackDetail.terminalState: Terminal state — clone to create a new pack.
compliancePackDetail.deleteHint: Cascade deletes all rules and phrases. Active packs must be deprecated first.
compliancePackDetail.deleteConfirm: Delete pack "{{name}}" and all its rules/phrases?
compliancePackDetail.regexWarning: JS RegExp warning (server uses Go RE2): {{message}}
compliancePackDetail.phrasesHint: One submission = one record. Paste multi-line text — detector splits by newline.
compliancePackDetail.phraseLabelHint: — one line per phrase. # comments and blank lines are ignored at detection time.
compliancePackDetail.phrasePlaceholder:
保证收益
稳赚不赔
# comments allowed
零风险
compliancePackDetail.lineCountHint_one: {{count}} non-blank/non-comment line will be split at detection time.
compliancePackDetail.lineCountHint_other: {{count}} non-blank/non-comment lines will be split at detection time.

compliancePage.pageDescription: Compliance events detected on this machine — your own view. Original text never leaves your device.
compliancePage.toggleOrgEnforced: Enforced by your organization policy — cannot be disabled
